Title:

Title:
What a special night! This year’s Spring ES Panther Choir Concert was a beautiful celebration of our ES Social Emotional Learning theme: “We Can Do Hard Things”—and your children proved it on stage!

With support from the ES Art Department, ES Strings, and the Middle and High School Choirs, the evening was filled with big dreams, brave voices, and joyful music. Each song shared a message of courage, teamwork, and determination.

Thank you for cheering them on. We look forward to seeing you next semester at our ES Panther Choir Festival of Lights!

#ISB #ISBThailand #ISBESMUSIC

Confidence was high in our boys varsity golf team this season as we geared up for IASAS in Kuala Lumpur. But these things are never easy—we lost one of our strongest players to illness the day before we travelled.
Strong performances in rounds one and two secured us a spot in the final against a tough TAS team. In a closely fought battle, four of the six matches went to the final hole, but our boys held their nerve and came out on top—clinching our third consecutive gold medal! 
Special shout-out to Kevin Xia, who made birdie on his final hole in round two to win his second consecutive individual gold!
So proud of all our players for showing incredible grit, skill, and staying calm under pressure.
#ISB #IASAS
#3Peat #Champions #BoysGolf #ProudTeam #KualaLumpur2025

Every child is different. At ISB, our teachers are trained to recognize unique strengths and guide students toward their version of success. 🎓 

From flexible learning strategies to purpose driven mentorship, we make sure no one gets left behind and everyone finds their place. With a combination of our passionate educators, and an environment that empowers self-discovery, we guide students to find their own voice, purpose, and potential. 🎵

Discover the difference at ISB, learn more. 

#ISB #GlobalEducation #LearningThroughGrowth #FindingPotential

What makes your child unique is exactly what we nurture at ISB. We meet learners where they are and help them discover what’s next; with care, flexibility, and the right challenge at every step. 👣

Our role is to guide, support, and inspire them to take ownership of that journey. With world class educators, diverse opportunities, and personalized learning, ISB empowers each student to grow into their fullest self whatever that looks like. 🎨🎤📹

#ISB #GlobalEducation #ISBPrograms #DiverseStudents

At ISB, our care extends far beyond our campus gates. This Back-to-School Blood Drive with the Thai Red Cross is a chance for us to stand together as a community and give the gift of life.

Every drop counts. In Thailand Rh negative blood is rare, and this is where our ISB community makes a powerful difference. By coming together, our international donors provide hope and life-saving support that reaches far beyond our campus gates. Each contribution is a reminder of the care, generosity, and unity that define ISB.

Join students, parents, staff, and alumni on August 18, 2025 at MPB Level 2 from 09:00 to 15:00 (with a lunch break from 12:00 to 13:00). Open to donors aged 17 to 70.

Let’s roll up our sleeves and show how ISB lifts others, together.

#ISBCommunity #BackToSchool #BloodDrive #ThaiRedCross #CareBeyondOurGates

Thank you to everyone who joined today’s Back to School Blood Drive with the Thai Red Cross.

Every drop counts and your generosity makes a life saving difference, especially here in Thailand where Rh negative blood is rare. Together, we showed once again that ISB care reaches far beyond our campus gates.

#ISBCommunity #BackToSchool #BloodDrive #ThaiRedCross #CareBeyondOurGates

#isb #isbthailand #ArtNow

MS Wai Kru Assembly 2025
On September 5, 2025, MS students and teachers gathered in the ISB Theatre for the Wai Kru Assembly, a cherished Thai tradition that honors teachers with gratitude and respect. The program featured the King’s Anthem, student MCs, the traditional teacher worship chant, floral garland presentations to new faculty, administrators, counselors, and instructional assistants, as well as student-created media expressing gratitude and a performance by the MS Choir.

This cultural assembly also encouraged students to be ready to learn for the new academic year, highlighting ISB values of Responsibility and Commitment. Teachers shared tips for success, while the program also brought plenty of energy and fun. Students enjoyed a lively hand-clap brain break and Ready to Learn: House Charades, where each House teamed up with their teachers on stage to act and guess words—building teamwork, integrity, and school spirit. The program closed with Principal Mr. Harter’s inspiring words on gratitude and the joy of learning.

The Wai Kru Assembly was a wonderful success, celebrating Thai culture, ISB values, and the strong bond between students and teachers. Well done, Middle School!

Title:
The new ISB Theater came alive on September 13 as we hosted the Bangkok International Choir Festival concert. Over 100 choir students from nine international schools — Bangkok Patana, Grace, ICS Nonthaburi, ISB, Jakarta Intercultural, Ruamrudee, Shrewsbury, Taipei American, and Thai-Chinese International — joined voices in harmony under the direction of Ms. Dinah Helgeson, former ISB Choir Director (1999–2010).

It was an unforgettable afternoon celebrating music, community, and collaboration. Thank you to everyone who joined us to support these talented young musicians.

#ISB #ISBTHAILAND

This week’s ISB MUN Showcase Debate tackled a timely and thought-provoking topic: the threat of bioterrorism amidst the rising accessibility of genome editing. The quality of speeches was outstanding — both in delivery and in depth of content.

Delegates, impeccably turned out in formal wear, carried themselves with the poise and professionalism of true diplomats.

In his Chair’s Report preceding the debate, ISB MUN Secretary General, Charlie Doherty, urged delegates to engage deeply with the issue, reminding them that “it is only by fostering collaboration between the scientific community, academia, individuals, public and private stakeholders, and the international community that we can come closer to utilizing the double-edged sword of biotechnology primarily for the benefit of humanity.”

The debate certainly embodied this spirit of collaboration — showcasing critical thinking, eloquence, and a shared commitment to ensuring biotechnology serves the progress, not the peril, of humankind.

#ISB #ISBThailand #ISBMUN #ISBHighSchool

The event held on November 1 was called Ennichi, which originally referred to a day when a deity or Buddha was believed to form a spiritual connection (En) with the human world. These days were considered opportunities for people to establish a bond with a specific deity or Buddha, believed to bring blessings or salvation. Over time, as crowds gathered at temples and shrines on such days, merchants began setting up markets and festivals, eventually giving rise to the lively Ennichi events filled with food stalls and games that we see today.

The Japanese Heritage Language and Culture club holds this Ennichi event every year to share Japanese culture and traditions with the ISB community. The event features Japanese snacks, foods, and traditional games. Thanks to the strong support from parents, it has become a lively and joyful annual event.

Last week, Grade 5 welcomed members of the UNHCR High School Club, who visited to share about their important work raising awareness of refugees in Thailand. The high school students spoke about the challenges many refugees face and how their club promotes understanding, empathy, and action within our school community.

In Literacy this semester, Grade 5 students have been exploring stories of hope and determination, reading both fiction and nonfiction texts about migrants and refugees, past and present. Meeting with the UNHCR Club allowed students to make meaningful connections between their classroom learning and real-world experiences.

This buddy opportunity not only deepens Grade 5 students’ understanding of global issues but also helps them see how young people can make a difference through kindness and advocacy. These partnerships with older students inspire elementary learners by showing positive role models who lead with empathy and purpose. At the same time, high school students gain valuable leadership and mentoring experience. Together, both groups are reminded of the power of compassion and the importance of standing up for others, near and far.

Loy Krathong is one of Thailand’s most cherished festivals, celebrated on the night of the full moon in the twelfth lunar month. It is a time for people to come together and float small, beautifully handcrafted boats called krathongs on water to honor the Goddess of Water. The floating of the krathongs symbolizes letting go of negativity, expressing gratitude, and welcoming good fortune for the year ahead.

At ISB, our students, parents, and community members gathered to learn about and celebrate this meaningful Thai tradition. The event was filled with creativity, reflection, and a sense of shared appreciation for the beauty of Thai culture.

Thank you to our students, parents, and the ISB community for joining us in celebrating Loy Krathong. We are grateful for the moments of connection and understanding that make our community so special.

#ISB #ISBTHAILAND #ISBCommunity #LoyKrathong

On 12 November, our community came together in quiet reflection as we honored the life and legacy of Her Majesty Queen Sirikit The Queen Mother. In the stillness of the ISB Theatre, students, educators, parents, and distinguished guests, including Ms. Supamas Isarabhakdi from the Prime Minister’s Office, gathered to celebrate a life devoted to culture, craftsmanship, and service.
At ISB, moments like this are more than ceremonies, they are opportunities for our international community to learn from, honor, and connect deeply with the cultural heritage of Thailand. Led by our students, the Royal Anthem opened the morning with dignity and gratitude, setting the tone for a shared experience rooted in respect and understanding across cultures.
Each part of the program - the Khon performance, student poetry and reflections, a commemorative film, and an ROTC Tribute - became a lens through which our diverse community could engage with Her Majesty’s enduring influence. Students and guests alike experienced not only the beauty of Thai tradition, but the power of learning side by side across languages, backgrounds, and perspectives.
Following the ceremony, our Royal Exhibition continued this exchange of culture and learning. Through traditional Thai dress, biography, and hands-on workshops in weaving and mask-making, students explored heritage in ways that encouraged curiosity, collaboration, and cross-cultural connection. These shared experiences help our learners understand one another and the world around them, more deeply.
In this way, honoring the Queen Mother reflects the heart of an ISB education. Every day, our students learn from one another’s cultures, listen with openness, and collaborate across differences. It is through these experiences that they grow into true global citizens, ready to thrive, contribute, and lead with understanding in an interconnected world.

#ISB #InternationalSchoolBangkok #QueenMother #ThaiCulture #ISBCommunity #HonoringTradition

On 21–22 November, ISB and fellow IASAS swim teams hosted the seventh annual “Best on Best” swim competition, welcoming the top athletes from the Asian Pacific Athletics Conference (APAC) to campus. ISB has now hosted this event four times, including the inaugural meet in 2016.

In this unique competition format, swimmers from each conference combine to form two mixed teams, setting aside their traditional school colors for the weekend. Historically, APAC has held a strong advantage, winning four of the previous six meets. This year followed the same pattern, with APAC securing a decisive victory over IASAS by a final score of 526 to 256.

Despite the overall result, ISB earned a notable highlight from its girls team, which broke seven school records and amassed the highest point total of any individual school squad, male or female, at the competition. New school records were set by freshman Manee Chen, junior Jenis Sunthornrangsri, and senior Napat Romsala.

A heartfelt thank you to all ISB students, parents, and our Booster Club for their truly spectacular support throughout the event as well as to all visiting athletes and families from other teams for making this event such a memorable and spirited occasion.

The December 2025 MS/HS Band Concert was themed A World of Harmony: Holiday Joy and Royal Remembrance, and featured performances by MS Concert Band, MS Symphonic Band, and HS Wind Ensemble in the new ISB theater. The program included music from all around the world, including His Majesty Rama IX's "Still on My Mind"—written for the late Queen Sirikit—as well highlights of the movie "La La Land," "Golden" from the hit movie KPop Demon Hunters, and many holiday songs, with a grand finale of "Waka Waka (This time for Africa)" performed by the combined MS Symphonic Band and HS Wind Ensemble. The 160 students made a fantastic wall of sound!

Over the past few months, ISB Guest Artist Khun Punch worked side-by-side with our high school ceramics students on an incredible collaborative ceramic tile relief sculpture. This long-term project reflects the creativity, dedication, and shared effort of our students and artist in residence.

Yesterday, we celebrated the installation of Finding Our Place, now proudly displayed in the Peace Garden for our community to enjoy. The opening was made even sweeter with cake and the joy of coming together to recognize this amazing student commission.

Our kindergarten students celebrated a major milestone this week: their 100th day of school! To mark the occasion, the children participated in various 100-themed activity rotations, practicing their skills through hands-on building and counting challenges. The classrooms also featured our "100 Collections Museum Walk," where students proudly displayed their collections of 100 items of their choice.

A highlight of the day was seeing the entire kindergarten grade gather to count to 100 together—a wonderful display of how much they have learned since the fall. The celebration ended on a sweet note with 100 cupcakes to share among the classes. We are so proud of our students' hard work over these first 100 days and look forward to even more growth throughout the rest of the year!

This was a highly competitive event featuring 70 teams from across Thailand, showcasing an inspiring level of innovation, engineering, and collaboration.
All eight of our teams earned team awards, including trophies in Robot Performance and CORE Values, recognizing their teamwork, creativity, and problem-solving. Two of our teams placed in the top 11 out of 70 teams in the Robot Game — an outstanding national achievement!
Beyond the awards, what stood out most was the incredible growth of our students. They:

✨ Persevered through technical challenges
✨ Presented their innovation projects confidently to judges
✨ Collaborated under pressure
✨ Represented ISB with integrity and enthusiasm

Most importantly, they had fun celebrating six months of learning, design, coding, and teamwork.
A heartfelt thank you to our dedicated Panther Robotics coaching team for their countless hours of support:
Mr. Marshall, Ms. Pool, Ms. Marshall, Mr. Przybylski, and Mr. Ciambrello.
We are so proud of our Panthers! 🐾💛

The 18 members of ISB's IASAS Speech and Debate team displayed their talents at our recent Speech and Debate Showcase. They will be heading off to Jakarta Intercultural School on Wednesday, Feb. 25th to compete against the best speakers from the other IASAS Schools in the disciplines of Original Oratory, Oral Interpretation, Impromptu Speaking, Extemporaneous Speaking and Debate.  Expectations are high after ISB's unprecedented return of 4 gold and 1 silver medal at last year's event in Taipei.

For seventy-five years, students have walked through these gates.
Teachers have opened doors.
Families from around the world have built community here in Bangkok.

ISB began with a simple belief — that education should prepare young people not just for exams, but for the world.

Over time, that belief shaped something lasting.

Friendships that stretch across continents.
Teachers who influence lives far beyond the classroom.
Students who grow into leaders, creators, innovators, and citizens of a global community.

On May 14, we will mark this milestone.

In the weeks ahead, we’ll be sharing stories from across decades — the people and moments that shaped ISB into what it is today.

If you’ve been part of ISB’s journey, we’d love to hear from you. Tell us your first ISB memory in the comments.

On March 18th, our youngest and oldest musicians came together in a celebration of music, movement and storytelling. The ES Panther Orff Ensemble and the HS Wind Ensemble demonstrated the beauty and joy that can arise when we approach music from a variety of perspectives and bring those perspectives together to interact with the same piece of music. In "Winter Milky Way", composed by Yukiko Nishimura, the music of the Wind Ensemble evoked images of a beautiful snowstorm, a fun-filled snow day, and restful slumber, enacted by the Panther Orff Ensemble. In "Variations on a Kitchen Sink", composed by Don Gillis, the Orff musicians participated in the Wind Ensemble percussion section, playfully imitating chefs at work in a busy kitchen and using actual kitchen utensils as percussion instruments. Throughout this special event, each ensemble inspired the other to appreciate the music and music making even more deeply, which in turn inspired the audience.
